content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum

To : Mickey                                                    Date : April 12, 1976

From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
       Carla

Subject: Mass mailings at public expense
         Government Code § 89001

     During the discussion of Government Code § 89001
at the Commission meeting last week, you asked me to
give you the following information.

     It was the sense of the Commission that mailings
made to persons who request that their names be placed
on mailing lists to receive information regarding
particular subjects are regarded as exceptions to the
mass mailing requirements.

cjb                                          Circulate to staff

[The image appears to be a scanned document that is extremely faint and largely illegible. Most of the text is too washed out or faded to read with confidence. There are some very faint marks and text scattered throughout the page, but attempting to transcribe them would result in significant guessing rather than accurate transcription.]

sections.conclusion: It was the sense of the Commission that mailings made to persons who request that their names be placed on mailing lists to receive information regarding particular subjects are regarded as exceptions to the mass mailing requirements.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether mass mailings to persons who have requested to be placed on mailing lists to receive information on particular subjects constitute an exception to Government Code § 89001 mass mailing requirements.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC determined that mailings sent to individuals who have voluntarily requested to be placed on mailing lists for specific subjects are exempt from Government Code § 89001 mass mailing restrictions.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is a memorandum format advice letter without formal QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections. Legal conclusion is embedded in body text. Addressed to Mickey regarding discussion held at Commission meeting. Initials 'cjb' indicate author.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether mass mailings to persons who have requested to be placed on mailing lists to receive information on particular subjects constitute an exception to Government Code § 89001 mass mailing requirements.

CONCLUSION: It was the sense of the Commission that mailings made to persons who request that their names be placed on mailing lists to receive information regarding particular subjects are regarded as exceptions to the mass mailing requirements.

embedding.summary: FPPC memorandum regarding Government Code § 89001 clarifying that mass mailings to persons who have requested placement on subject-specific mailing lists are considered exceptions to mass mailing requirements.
